har gow
Cantonese name literally translates to shrimp dumpling
cioppino
Italian-American seafood stew defined by a mix of shellfish including crab and shrimp
mam tom
Vietnamese fermented shrimp paste (mam tom) that is the defining ingredient of dishes bearing this name
etouffee
classic Cajun/Creole preparation is crawfish or shrimp based
som tam
Thai green papaya salad traditionally dressed with dried shrimp and shrimp paste (kapi)
char kway teow
stir-fry traditionally includes shrimp and/or cockles alongside Chinese sausage
laksa
Malaysian/Nyonya laksa broths commonly built with dried shrimp and/or prawn paste
tom yum
classic broth (tom yum goong) is shrimp-based; other proteins are less traditional variants
xo sauce
Hong Kong condiment made from dried shrimp and dried scallop
fideua
Spanish seafood noodle dish traditionally includes shrimp/prawns
green curry paste
Thai curry paste base is traditionally pounded with shrimp paste (kapi)
pad thai
traditional tamarind sauce is typically built with dried shrimp and/or shrimp paste, even in chicken/veg versions
siu mai
traditional Cantonese filling commonly blends pork with shrimp
massaman
curry paste base traditionally includes shrimp paste, though many restaurant pastes omit it
panang curry
curry paste base traditionally includes shrimp paste, though modern jarred pastes vary
wonton
traditional Cantonese filling often blends shrimp with pork, though pork-only versions are common too
bisque
classically a shellfish (lobster/crab/shrimp) stock-based soup, but many modern menu 'bisques' (tomato, squash) contain none
